title: Versioning of inferred data in an enriched isolated collection of resources and relationships
abstract: Examples of the present disclosure describe systems and methods that control versioning of inferred data in isolated collections representing resources and relationships between those resources. Multiple versions of rulesets may be received or accessed. Execution of each ruleset generates inferred data, such as inferred relationships between resources represented in the isolated collection. A version identifier may be incorporated into each inferred relationship or other inferred data. The version identifier indicates the particular version of a ruleset that resulted in the creation of the corresponding inferred relationship. Upon receiving a query for data in the isolated collection, the query is parsed to determine a query version identifier. Based on the query version identifier, results to the query may be filtered to include inferred data resulting only from a particular version of a ruleset. The results may be displayed as a graph with nodes representing resources and edges representing relationships.
claims: Claims 1. A system comprising: at least one processor; and a memory storing instructions that when executed by the at least one processor perform a set of operations comprising: accessing an isolated collection of asserted resources and asserted relationships between the asserted resources; accessing a first version of a ruleset for the isolated collection, wherein one or more rules in the ruleset are based on values of the asserted relationships; executing the first version of the ruleset against the isolated collection to generate a first plurality of inferred relationships between the asserted resources in the isolated collection; generate a first version identifier for each inferred relationship within the first plurality, wherein the version identifier indicates the first version of the ruleset; receiving a second version of the ruleset for the isolated collection; executing the second version of the ruleset against the isolated collection to generate a second plurality of inferred relationships between the asserted resources in the isolated collection; and generating a second version identifier for each inferred relationship within the first plurality, wherein the version identifier indicates the first version of the ruleset. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the operations further comprise: receiving a query against the isolated collection; parsing the query to extract a query version identifier; processing the query against the isolated collection to generate results to the query; based on the query version identifier, identifying a version of the ruleset corresponding to the query; and filtering the results based on the identified version such that any inferred relationships included in the results were produced by processing the identified version of the ruleset. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the operations further comprise displaying the filtered results as a graph showing each of the asserted resources as nodes within the graph and each of the asserted and inferred relationships as edges between the nodes. 4. A computer-implemented method for handling versioning of rulesets, the method comprising: accessing an isolated collection comprising at least two or more asserted resources and one or more relationships between the asserted resources; accessing a first version of a ruleset for the isolated collection, wherein one or more rules in the ruleset are based on values of the asserted relationships; executing the first version of the ruleset against the isolated collection to generate a first plurality of inferred relationships between the asserted resources in the isolated collection; generate a first version identifier for each inferred relationship within the first plurality, wherein the version identifier indicates the first version of the ruleset; receiving a second version of the ruleset for the isolated collection; executing the second version of the ruleset against the isolated collection to generate a second plurality of inferred relationships between the asserted resources in the isolated collection; and generating a second version identifier for each inferred relationship within the first plurality, wherein the version identifier indicates the first version of the ruleset. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 4, further comprising: receiving a query against the isolated collection; parsing the query to extract a query version identifier; executing the query against the isolated collection to generate results to the query; comparing the query version identifier to the first version identifier and the second version identifier; based on the comparison, identifying a version of the ruleset corresponding to the query; and filtering the results based on the identified version such that any inferred relationships included in the results were produced by executing the identified version of the ruleset. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 5, further comprising displaying the filtered results as a graph showing each of the asserted resources as nodes within the graph and each of the asserted and inferred relationships as edges between the nodes. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 5, wherein the query version indicator is one of an application program interface version number, a ruleset version number, or a ruleset version name. 8. A computer-implemented method for handling versioning of rulesets, the method comprising: receiving a query against an isolated collection of asserted resources and asserted relationships between the asserted resources; parsing the query to extract a query version identifier; based on the query version identifier, selecting a version of a ruleset to process against the isolated collection; executing the query by processing the selected version of the ruleset against the isolated collection to generate a plurality of inferred relationships between the asserted resources in the isolated collection; and returning results of the query, wherein the results of the query include at least a portion of the asserted resources and the inferred relationships. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein the query version identifier is included as a part of a Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) header or a JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) parameter. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, further comprising caching the inferred relationships in the isolated collection for a period of time to complete execution of the query. 11. The system of claim 2, wherein the query version indicator is one of an application program interface (API) version number, a ruleset version number, or at least a portion of a ruleset version name. 12. The system of claim 1, wherein the first version identifier is stored as metadata of the respective inferred relationship. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 4, wherein the first version identifier is one of a ruleset version number or at least a portion of a name the first version of the ruleset. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 5, wherein the query version identifier is received as a part of a Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) header or a JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) parameter. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, further comprising recording the frequency of a value of the query version indicator for the query and subsequent queries in a frequency metric dataset.
